evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: establish a single source of truth for business metric definitions; trusted semantic layer; well-documented data definitions
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: Classified as rigour because responsibilities centre on establishing 'a single source of truth', building a 'trusted semantic layer', and producing 'well-documented data definitions' — with no velocity signals present.
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: Classified as moderate because Awin is an affiliate marketing platform where errors affect business decisions without regulatory or direct financial reporting consequence.
evidence.collaboration_width: Data Engineering teams; BI Developers; Insight Analysts
evidence.data_team_maturity: establish their analytics engineering practice within the BI team
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: Classified as mid because the analytics engineering practice is being established within an existing BI team that already has BI Developers and Insight Analysts — growing, not zero.
evidence.jd_authorship: provide business context for the mart layers of our data architecture; coach BI Developers in Analytics Engineering best practices
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: Classified as hiring_manager because the JD names specific architectural layers (mart layers, semantic layer), a concrete coaching mission, and the exact problem being solved (single source of truth).
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: self-serve analytics across the organisation; coach BI Developers; enable independent contributions
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: Classified as internal_data because the primary output is the analytics engineering practice itself — serving BI Developers and Insight Analysts as internal data consumers.
evidence.autonomy_level: establish their analytics engineering practice; establish a single source of truth for business metric definitions
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: Classified as strategic because 'establish the analytics engineering practice' and 'establish a single source of truth' signal the candidate is defining the practice from the ground up, not executing against a set brief.
evidence.ai_role_explanation: 'AI-readiness concepts' appears in preferred qualifications as a vague phrase without specifying what the candidate is expected to do with AI — classified as none.
evidence.testing_framing: exposure to data observability or AI-readiness concepts
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: Classified as tool_listed because data observability appears in preferred qualifications without any ownership verb framing — it is a nice-to-have skill, not a named candidate responsibility.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing: trusted semantic layer; single source of truth
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: Classified as moderate because 'trusted semantic layer' and 'single source of truth' imply preventing metric inconsistency, but the framing is delivery-oriented with no compliance or audit language.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: establish their analytics engineering practice within the BI team
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: Classified as mixed because the analytics engineering practice is being established from scratch (greenfield) within an existing BI team that already has infrastructure (fix_scale).
evidence.loss_aversion: trusted semantic layer; single source of truth — implied risk is metric inconsistency across teams, but no compliance or trust-failure framing present.
